---
title: "A Beautiful Graphic"
author: "Oluchi Nnadi"
format: html
execute: 
  echo: false
---

Oluchi Nnadi is a data maestro!

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Create the base plot with mpg dataset
ggplot(data = mpg, aes(x = displ, y = hwy, color = drv)) +
  
  # 2. Add points with some transparency (alpha) to handle overlapping data
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # 3. Add a smooth trend line for each drivetrain category without the standard error ribbon
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, size = 1.2, formula = y ~ x) +
  
  # 4. Apply a beautiful, modern color palette (Viridis)
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "plasma", end = 0.8, 
                        labels = c("4" = "4-Wheel", "f" = "Front-Wheel", "r" = "Rear-Wheel")) +
  
  # 5. Use a clean, minimalist background theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  
  # 6. Customize labels and titles
  labs(
    title = "Engine Displacement vs. Highway Fuel Efficiency",
    subtitle = "Front-wheel drive cars remain the most efficient as engine size grows",
    x = "Engine Displacement (Liters)",
    y = "Highway Miles per Gallon (MPG)",
    color = "Drivetrain Type",
    caption = "Data Source: EPA (mpg dataset)"
  ) +
  
  # 7. Fine-tune the theme for maximum visual appeal
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, color = "#222222"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "#555555", margin = margin(b = 15)),
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "#888888", face = "italic"),
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), # Remove distracting minor grid lines
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "#eaeaea")
  )
```
